intro: Translating JavaScript codebases into C++ shifts an architecture from a managed web platform engine to a high-performance compiled environment. Developers adopt this pathway to create low-latency systems, resource-critical game architectures, or complex desktop graphics modules. This conversion demands strict attention to manual memory constraints, data typing hierarchies, and deterministic object lifecycles.

diffs.from: Dynamic runtime types utilizing automatic coercion paths and structural duck typing definitions.
diffs.to: Statically checked, nominal typing supporting explicit class structures, templates, and compile-time evaluation.

diffs.from: Flexible scripts utilizing arrow notations, direct object literals, and implicit semicolon insertions.
diffs.to: Rigid C++ layout requiring explicit header declarations, scope resolution operators, and manual template parameterizations.

diffs.from: Automated engine-level heap management using non-deterministic background garbage collection routines.
diffs.to: Deterministic resource management driven by RAII, smart pointers (std::unique_ptr, std::shared_ptr), and scoped destruction timelines.

diffs.from: Built-in asynchronous engine loop managing promises, microtasks, and continuous callback executions.
diffs.to: Native system concurrency driven by std::thread, asynchronous task futures, condition variables, and mutex lock abstractions.

diffs.from: Tailored for browser DOM structures, V8 runtime contexts, and text network data conversions.
diffs.to: The Standard Template Library (STL), which provides low-level memory allocators, optimized algorithmic patterns, and generic containers.

examples.title: Object Structure and Methods
examples.description: Converting dynamic object instantiation into an idiomatic RAII class layout.

examples.title: Array Filtering
examples.description: Transforming a functional array filter loop into a standard C++ STL vector algorithm copy operation.

tips.title: Prefer Smart Pointers
tips.body: Avoid using raw 'new' or 'delete' operators. Instead, rely on std::unique_ptr and std::shared_ptr to eliminate memory leaks.

tips.title: Utilize Auto Deduction
tips.body: Maintain clean syntax definitions by using the 'auto' keyword to declare complex nested type layouts.

tips.title: Pass Large Data by Reference
tips.body: Prevent costly overhead copy operations by passing complex structures into functions using const references (const T&).

tips.title: Enforce Const Correctness
tips.body: Mark all non-mutating instance functions with the 'const' modifier to enable maximum optimization by the compiler.

faqs.question: What replaces JavaScript arrays in C++?
faqs.answer: Use std::vector for dynamically resizing array needs, or std::array if the allocation constraints require fixed static lengths.

faqs.question: How do I emulate dynamic keys without a defined type?
faqs.answer: You can utilize std::unordered_map<std::string, std::any> to replicate loose dynamic dictionary indexing maps.

faqs.question: Does C++ have an event loop like JavaScript?
faqs.answer: No, C++ executes code sequentially by default. To match event loops, you must build one manually or import external toolkits like boost::asio.
